items.headline: Minnesota Wild lineup updates ahead of game 3 against Colorado Avalanche
items.description:
ST. PAUL, Minn. -- Goalie Jesper Wallstedt will return and start for the Minnesota Wild in Game 3 of the Western Conference Second Round against the Colorado Avalanche at Grand Casino Arena on Saturday.
Center Joel Eriksson Ek will miss his third straight game and defenseman Jonas Brodin his fourth,

items.headline: TNT analyst Boucher says extra rest could help Minnesota against Colorado in game 3
items.description:
ST. PAUL, Minn. -- Three full days between Games 2 and 3 in the Western Conference Second Round could be the best medicine for the Minnesota Wild, according to TNT broadcast anaylst Brian Boucher.
The obvious reason is rest, especially for defensemen Quinn Hughes and Brock Faber, who averaged 28:32

items.headline: Carolina Hurricanes Philadelphia Flyers Game 4 preview May 9, 2026
items.description:

items.headline: Stanley Cup Playoffs Playback for May 9 2026
items.description: The Stanley Cup Playoffs are in full swing and NHL.com has it all covered. Each day, we will present Stanley Cup Playoffs Playback, your one-stop shop for all the action. Here’s a look at where things stand on Saturday, May 9:

items.headline: Anaheim Ducks doomed by slow start in Game 3 loss
items.description:
ANAHEIM -- The Anaheim Ducks have shown they are capable of flight in the 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs.
Now, they’ll need to demonstrate they can pick themselves up and dust themselves off after losing 6-2 to the Vegas Golden Knights in Game 3 of the Western Conference Second Round here

items.headline: Mitch Marner keeps focus on own voice in helping Golden Knights
items.description: ANAHEIM -- The Vegas Golden Knights are discovering two special traits Mitch Marner possesses beyond his ability to play hockey at an all-world level.

items.headline: Vegas Golden Knights Anaheim Ducks Game 3 recap May 8, 2026
items.description: ANAHEIM -- Mitch Marner had a natural hat trick and assisted on a short-handed goal for the Vegas Golden Knights, who defeated the Anaheim Ducks 6-2 in Game 3 of the Western Conference Second Round at Honda Center on Friday.

items.headline: Alex Newhook relentless with two goals in Canadiens Game 2 win
items.description: BUFFALO -- A shirtless Alex Newhook looked like a man possessed as he stormed around the Montreal Canadiens dressing room screaming out the names of the team’s opening lineup prior to Game 2 of the Eastern Conference Second Round against the Buffalo Sabres at KeyBank Center on Friday.

items.headline: 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs second round schedule television results
items.description: The second round of the 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs began Saturday with the Carolina Hurricanes defeating the Philadelphia Flyers 3-0. The second round continued on Sunday with the Colorado Avalanche defeating the Minnesota Wild 9-6.

items.headline: Anaheim Ducks Radko Gudas injury status update second round NHL playoffs
items.description:
ANAHEIM -- Radko Gudas could return to the Anaheim Ducks lineup at some point during the Western Conference Second Round, coach Joel Quenneville said Friday.
“He could be possible for the series,” Quenneville said following the Ducks’ morning skate at Honda Center ahead of their 6-2 loss to the Vegas

items.headline: Tage Thompson ‘fighting it’ as playoff struggles continue
items.description:
BUFFALO – Everywhere in Game 2, disaster was befalling Tage Thompson.
The star forward for the Buffalo Sabres was making wrong decisions, unable to settle the puck on his stick, falling at the offensive blue line on the back-breaking goal in a 5-1 loss at KeyBank Center on Friday.
“Just

items.headline: Montreal Canadiens Buffalo Sabres game 2 recap May 8 2026
items.description: BUFFALO -- Alex Newhook scored twice, and the Montreal Canadiens defeated the Buffalo Sabres 5-1 in Game 2 of the Eastern Conference Second Round at KeyBank Center on Friday.

items.headline: Ryan Miller fires up Buffalo Sabres fans before Game 2 East 2nd Round
items.description:
Ryan Miller returned to familiar grounds on Friday.
The former Buffalo Sabres goalie banged the drum before the team’s game against the Montreal Canadiens in Game 2 of the Eastern Conference Second Round at KeyBank Center.
Sporting his No. 30 Sabres jersey, Miller motioned for the crowd to get louder

items.description:
